creator: fenner@parc.xerox.com
text:
In message <199701210957.BAA02858@stanton-1-1.quick.net>you write:
># ex:ts=8

Just FYI, this is what ex/vi traditionally calls a "modeline", and sets modes for individual files (like *Text* or whatever does for Emacs).  However, FreeBSD's vi does not (and will never) implement it because it's a security hole.

I agree that it doesn't belong there.

  Bill
